BIT-001.title: BitLocker is disabled
BIT-001.description: The operating system drive is not protected with BitLocker encryption.
BIT-001.risk: Data can be accessed if the device is lost or stolen.
BIT-001.recommendation: Enable BitLocker for the system drive.

DEF-001.title: Windows Defender is disabled
DEF-001.description: Microsoft Defender Antivirus is not enabled or its state is unknown.
DEF-001.risk: Malware may execute without endpoint protection detecting or blocking it.
DEF-001.recommendation: Turn on Microsoft Defender Antivirus and verify protection status.

FW-001.title: Windows Firewall profile is disabled
FW-001.description: One or more Windows Firewall profiles are not enabled.
FW-001.risk: Unrestricted inbound connectivity can increase exposure to network attacks.
FW-001.recommendation: Enable Domain, Private, and Public firewall profiles unless an approved exception exists.

ADM-001.title: Local administrators require review
ADM-001.description: Local administrator membership is missing or exceeds the expected baseline.
ADM-001.risk: Excessive local administrator rights can enable privilege escalation and persistence.
ADM-001.recommendation: Review local administrators and keep only approved required accounts.

NET-001.title: Public network profile detected
NET-001.description: The active network connection is using the Public profile.
NET-001.risk: A wrong network profile can apply unsuitable firewall and sharing behavior.
NET-001.recommendation: Use Private or Domain profile when appropriate.

UPD-001.title: Windows Updates are missing or stale
UPD-001.description: The last successful update installation is missing or older than the accepted threshold.
UPD-001.risk: Missing security updates can leave known vulnerabilities exploitable.
UPD-001.recommendation: Install current Windows updates and verify successful installation telemetry.
